publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 14-15. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. Stephen will go by the 3 o'clock coach. As he has things to take with him, I consider this preferable to the Rail.
I am much dismayed about the Hen, who is still very ill. But I hope we shall get off on Weddy.-- Stephen will tell you where the
little parcel is. He knows exactly.

I am very well pleased with Miss Jane. She is very handsome; quite remarkably so. The head especially;-- & she seems gentle & affectionate. She is in height 15-2, & very compact. But I was quite in a taking at first, on discovering a larg swelling in one hock, & in one knee.

my relief, on investigation, that it is nothing but humour; & that she is in most foul & wretched grass condition, with only what corn in [two words crossed out] her she got on the journey. She is altogether in most unfavorable condition; the body much swelled, & the whole of her quite untrimmed & undressed. I am assured
she will do me more work than most two horses would, as soon as she is in condition. My mother thinks her a wonderful bargain, & most promising.
So Mrs L. King has a daughter!-- The more the merrier, male or female.
I do not favor your idea of teaching B--. I will write you more of my reasons: I must now put this up.
